title: Arn Horada

{{Quote|No, I'm from your ''past.'' When you traveled from here to ''Alderaan'' to instruct a little girl in galactic history-- ''Leia,'' Professor Horada. Princess Leia Organa.|Leia Organa to Arn Horada|<ref name="marvel30" />}}
'''Arn Horada''' was a renowned [[Historian/Legends|historian]] and [[Professor/Legends|professor]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history, who during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] served as a personal teacher to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] during her youth. A native of [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]], Horada eventually returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], where he worked as a common laborer and underwent extensive re-education under the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] government. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Organa traveled to Metalorn in the hopes of re-establishing a link with Horada and helping to establish a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] presence on the [[Planet/Legends|world]]. Although her plot was uncovered, Horada openly defied the Empire and helped her escape the planet.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Why the Alliance would let its most renowned leader risk her life attempting to recruit some broken, useless former historian eludes me... I'll let you explain that to the Emperor... when I deliver you personally!|Orman Tagge, to Leia Organa|<ref name="marvel30" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Arn Horada was a native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]], a factory world<ref name="marvel30" /> in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Horada eventually became a well-known [[Historian/Legends|historian]] acquiring a strong enough reputation that in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] royal [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] brought Horada to Alderaan to serve as a personal teacher for his young daughter [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]].<ref name="marvel30" /> By his time on Alderaan, Horada was regarded as one of the greatest [[Professor/Legends|professors]] and historians in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="db">{{DB|character|leiaorganasolo|Solo, Princess Leia Organa}}</ref> and educated the young Organa in [[Politics/Legends|politics]] and galactic history.<ref name="db2">{{DB|character|bailorgana|Organa, Bail}}</ref>

[[File:ChairKick.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Arn Horada trips Orman Tagge, helping Leia Organa escape Metalorn.]]
Afterwards, Horada returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], which saw a radical societal transformation after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rose to power in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Control of Metalorn fell into the hands of [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Corwyth]], who tightly regimented daily life on the planet and instituted an [[information control program]] that succeeded in convincing the planet at large that a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] did not even exist.<ref name="marvel30" /> The new Imperial government spent years re-educating Horada in particular, hoping that the respected historian could serve as an Imperial mouthpiece.<ref name="egtw">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> Despite his profile, Horada toiled as an ordinary laborer in Metalorn's factories, and in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] he learned of Alderaan's [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]], which Metalorn's central information bureau attributed to a [[Meteorite/Legends|meteor]] storm: according to them, none of the [[House of Organa/Legends|royal house]] he once served survived.<ref name="marvel30" />

In truth, Leia—now one of the Rebel Alliance's top leaders—had escaped her homeworld's obliteration, and several months later she traveled to Metalorn in the hopes of contacting her old teacher and using him to help set up a local resistance against Imperial rule. Organa's arrival didn't go unnoticed for long by the Imperial government's extensive surveillance system, but the Alderaanian [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] managed to escape pursuit long enough to make contact with Horada, who was shocked at her sudden appearance. However, their conversation was almost immediately interrupted by [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]], the head of the Imperial-allied [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]], who had learned of Horada's connection to Organa and correctly guessed the Rebel leader's objective. When Tagge attacked Organa, Horada kicked a chair into Tagge's path, giving his former student the opening she needed to flee. Humiliated by Organa's escape, Corwyth considered executing Horada for his interference, but ultimately let the historian walk free when Tagge suggested that any reprisal would only confirm the existence of Rebel activity.<ref name="marvel30" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|N-no! Are you trying to get me into trouble...? Alderaan is destroyed... obliterated by a meteor storm! Central information told us that long ago...! None of the royal house survived... none!|Arn Horada, to Leia Organa|<ref name="marvel30" />}}
Although a renowned historian and teacher, Arn Horada proved to be no less susceptible to the Empire's re-education efforts than most other natives of Metalorn, believing their false narratives of Alderaan's destruction and initially suspecting that Organa's appearance was some sort of trick. However, he still proved to have some fight in him, openly defying the Empire by interfering with Orman Tagge and allowing Organa to escape.<ref name="marvel30" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arn Horada first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 30|''Star Wars'' (1977) 30]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]], where he was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel30" />
